speaker: SPEAKER_02

speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: If you like Kotlin, I think that that's an advantage for Redwood and Zipline.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: In terms of tooling, Treehouse is built by mobile developers, kind of with mobile developers in mind.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: So you're not dealing with like NPM or VS Code if you don't want to.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: It works great with IntelliJ or Android Studio.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And yeah, part of the reason we went down this route as well is we think it has the greatest chance of convincing our organization of using kind of a shared code solution.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Like I mentioned, we have a lot of mobile developers.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And I think folks are kind of more palatable to Kotlin versus introducing a bunch of JavaScript into our code base.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And...
speaker: SPEAKER_02

speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: So we can use it for things as small as one view in the app.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

speaker: SPEAKER_02
